methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $110M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Manna Aero is a Series B-stage drone and autonomous systems company that has raised $110M to date. The relevant comp universe consists of 7 private companies in the Drones & Autonomous sector, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air; no direct public comparables exist in this narrowly-defined category.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable and forms the basis of valuation. Using total capital raised ($110M) as a proxy for post-money valuation, we apply a typical Series B-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x, yielding an implied valuation of $209M. The Peer-Median approach is not applicable: while four same-sector peers exist, filtering by stage and valuation tier yields insufficient datapoints (fewer than three required for statistical reliability), making a sector-wide median potentially misleading.

The triangulated analysis, constrained to a single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $178M (low) to $240M (high), with a median of $209M. The width of this range reflects standard variance around stage-progression multiples rather than convergence across independent methods. Key uncertainties include the absence of tightly-comparable private benchmarks at Manna's specific stage and market position, and reliance on historical step-up multiples that may not reflect current market conditions in the autonomous systems sector. Further valuation refinement would require either identification of additional same-stage comparables or access to transaction multiples within the drone ecosystem.
